title: Documentation State
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Records the verified permanent-document inventory, reconciliation status, control rules, risks, and remaining documentation work for TMS-OS.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document is the permanent control record for the current state of Two Marshalls Studios documentation.

It records the verified permanent-document inventory, the current reconciliation milestone, the status of core control documents, source-of-truth roles, remaining work, risks, and completion requirements.
It does not replace DOC-INDEX-001, which catalogs the documents themselves, or STATE-001, which records the operating state of TMS-OS.

sections.title: Core Documentation Rule
paragraphs: No documentation item is considered permanently established merely because a file exists, a draft was generated, or a document rendered successfully.

Permanent documentation must have a verified identity, an approved responsibility, valid structured content, a controlled destination, successful tests, an authorized session decision, and an auditable change history.

Repository evidence, permanent records, controlled tests, and explicit human approval govern documentation truth. Chat, screenshots, ZIP files, local exports, and memory may provide evidence, but must not remain the only location of important studio knowledge.

rows: Paused until core documentation reconciliation closes
Structurally complete; content reconciliation in progress

sections.title: Verified Permanent Document Inventory
paragraphs: The repository contains 20 structured permanent-document JSON files under pages/documents. No permanent JSON document was identified for immediate removal or merger during the WS086 inventory.
The repository also contains ARCH-001.html as a legacy companion file. ARCH-001.json remains the canonical structured record for the shared document viewer.

rows: DEC-LOG-001, MILE-HIST-001, WS-HIST-001, HIST-001, BUG-HIST-001, LESSON-001

sections.title: Documentation Architecture Assessment
paragraphs: The WS086 repository audit determined that the permanent-document architecture is structurally sound.
The primary issue is documentation freshness and cross-document reconciliation, not missing document categories.

Existing documents already cover current state, roadmap, architecture, documentation control, standards, procedures, history, decisions, risks, technical debt, lessons, bugs, milestones, work sessions, and enhancements.
items: Preserve the existing 20-document structured library.
Reconcile stale content rather than replace the documentation architecture.
Avoid duplicate governance documents unless a specific unowned responsibility is proven.
Keep strategic direction, current state, architecture, documentation health, and historical records separate.
Populate related-document relationships where useful and supported.

sections.title: Permanent Document Lifecycle
paragraphs: Every permanent-document change must follow a controlled lifecycle. Installing a replacement for testing does not by itself make that replacement permanently accepted.
items: Identify the document responsibility and verified source evidence.
Read the existing document and preserve its compatible schema.
Reconcile duplicate, conflicting, stale, or unsupported content.
Create the complete replacement or append proposal.
Validate JSON structure or required file format.
Install only the approved target file.
Verify document catalog access and viewer rendering.
Verify search behavior and browser Console state.
Verify Git change scope and absence of unrelated changes.
Record tests, decisions, files, risks, lessons, and remaining work in the active session.
Prepare the session review package.
Obtain explicit human approval before permanent closeout.
Commit and push only after approved closure.
Update dependent current, index, history, and register documents as required.

sections.title: Writer and Execution Boundary
paragraphs: The repository includes a permanent-document writer framework, five registered writers, transaction-analysis modules, controlled-output modules, rollback preparation, lifecycle controllers, and state-transition controls.

The presence of these modules does not establish that permanent writes or rollback execution are enabled. Draft generation, approval, authorization, execution, verification, rollback, and state recording remain separate responsibilities.
Until later controlled verification proves otherwise, documentation must preserve the disabled permanent-execution safety boundary recorded by the repository architecture map.

sections.title: Current Documentation Risks
items: Treating a rendered draft as a permanently accepted document.
Allowing core documents to disagree about the current milestone, phase, architecture, or next action.
Leaving current-state statements frozen at older work sessions.
Confusing the 20 structured repository documents with earlier recovery-package counts or Google publication counts.
Assuming external Google Headquarters contents were reverified when WS086 audited only the repository copy.
Maintaining duplicate or competing roadmap and architecture records without reconciliation.
Failing to reconcile the legacy docs/roadmap/ROADMAP.md after the permanent control documents are aligned.
Allowing important reasoning to remain only in chat.
Activating permanent writes, rollback, or state transitions based solely on module existence.
Adding deferred governance layers or enhancements during the current milestone without approval.
Committing unrelated files together with controlled documentation replacements.

sections.title: Deferred Governance Proposals
paragraphs: The following proposed documents remain deferred because the audit did not prove that their responsibilities are currently unowned:
items: VISION-001
CONSTITUTION-001
PROGRAMS-001
CATALOG-001
GOVERNANCE-TRANSITION-001

sections.title: Documentation Relationship Rules
items: STATE-001 answers where the project is now.
ARCH-001 answers how the current system is built.
ROADMAP-001 answers where approved work goes next.
DOC-STATE-001 answers how healthy and current the documentation system is.
DOC-INDEX-001 answers which permanent documents exist and how they are cataloged.
ARCH-EVO-001, HIST-001, WS-HIST-001, and MILE-HIST-001 preserve change over time.
DEC-LOG-001 preserves approved decisions.
BUG-HIST-001 and LESSON-001 preserve verified problems, corrections, and learning.
RISK-001 and TECH-DEBT-001 preserve unresolved exposure.
ENH-LOG-001 preserves future ideas without activating them.

sections.title: Documentation Reconciliation Completion Standard
paragraphs: The Documentation Reconciliation milestone is complete only when the permanent documentation set accurately reflects the verified repository and approved history, required relationships are consistent, and the Studio Portal presents the reconciled documents without new errors.
items: All core control documents reconciled and approved.
DOC-INDEX-001 matches the verified permanent-document inventory.
Standards and procedures match the implemented governance workflow.
Historical and register documents are current through the reconciliation closeout.
All JSON documents validate.
Document catalog entries resolve to the correct files.
The shared viewer renders all reconciled documents.
Search returns supported document results.
No new browser Console errors are introduced.
Git change scope contains only approved files.
Current, strategic, architectural, documentation-state, and historical statements do not conflict.
Prepare Session validation passes.
Human Approve & Close authorization is recorded.
Commit and push complete only after approved closure.

sections.title: Current Next Controlled Action
paragraphs: Install and verify this DOC-STATE-001 replacement.
After successful validation, reconcile DOC-INDEX-001 against the verified 20-document structured library.
Do not resume feature development or activate a new software milestone until the Documentation Reconciliation completion standard is met and the milestone is explicitly closed.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The Two Marshalls Studios documentation system is structurally established and governed by permanent identities, controlled responsibilities, repository evidence, shared rendering, work-session review, human approval, recoverability, and auditable history.

Its current obligation is reconciliation: preserve verified architecture and history, correct stale current information, prevent duplicate governance, and ensure that no important studio knowledge depends solely on chat, memory, or one workstation.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified recovered documentation-state record to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

Reconciled documentation state with the verified 20-document repository inventory, established the Documentation Reconciliation milestone status, defined remaining work and completion standards, and clarified source-of-truth and execution boundaries.

verification.method: Reconciled against the uploaded studio-portal repository, the verified pages/documents inventory, the existing DOC-STATE-001 record, the approved WS086 project state, and the prepared STATE-001, ARCH-001, and ROADMAP-001 reconciliation packages.
